rationale.R1.1: The section makes three explicit contribution claims (learned router, uncertainty calibration, deployment system) but the body only substantiates one of them (the confidence threshold router); the calibration and deployment claims are asserted but not demonstrated in this section, making contribution-claim coherence a real review target.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple logical gaps are load-bearing: (a) max(p) thresholding is presented as the routing policy yet contribution (1) promises a 'learned router' — these are inconsistent; (b) contribution (2) names a 'novel uncertainty calibration procedure based on pathology co-occurrence priors' but the procedure never appears in the body and is deferred to future work; (c) τ selection by 'minimizing escalation rate at fixed sensitivity' is under-specified — which sensitivity level, on which pathology?
rationale.R1.3: The AUROC figure (0.889 ± 0.004) and inference-time figures (4.2 → 1.6 s) are stated without a table or figure reference; it is unverifiable whether they come from the CheXpert validation split or a test split, and only 5 of 14 pathology classes have reported results, making aggregate AUROC coverage incomplete.
